column36: Went for zero escapes, so had to sacrifice speed, not sure if best decision.

Game 2 was won purely by a turn 3 Protector of the Universe! Being able to Danger Sense 4 every 4 turns at least was absolutely huge. It meant we delayed Charges and Twists until we could deal with them and arranged Fateful Resurrection to always fail. Still took a while, and all the Sidekicks meant a low score, but we kept it to 1 escape which Hyperion forced on his only Strike.
Game 1 was fine but with badly timed Strikes and Twist chains leading to 7 escapes, and that wasn't counting Hyperion twice, Wonder Woman and Batman who both left the City early.
It was a lovely puzzle to solve, especially with so many Heroes to choose from, and yet I still wish I'd leveraged Danger Sense more!!!

column36: I had a feeling this was going to be tough to avoid escapes with the amount of charging villains and mastermind. I was hoping captain marvel would have ramped up faster but she did the job eventually.

Really tough setup with the amount of charging and scheme twists pushing sidekicks into the city. Had no chance the first game but luckily less early scheme twists and master strikes in the second which made it possible. I did like my hero choices at least felt like everyone had some synergy with eachother

Went with Quasar and Yondu to try to work with Mar-Vell on shards and Danger Sense. First game all the strikes came out pretty early, with the city pretty empty. With Danger Sense I was able to push twists until the city could handle them, managing no villain escapes overall. I had trouble getting both of my hands going for attack though, and that game took 39 turns, and only had a PPT of 2.077 (81 points).

Second game was very different. I tried the same heroes again. 4 villains escaped in the first 10 turns, including 2 sidekicks. I didn't think the game would go better after that, but I kept playing. Surprisingly, Lady Thor ended up getting huge combos this game. It was a tough call picking this game over the no escapes round, but with 6 fewer turns and much better PPT, my hope is everyone else just had a ton of escapes.

Our hero choice doubled down on danger sense to manage the scheme twists and master strike charges to survive.  Getting Captain Mar-vel's rare with 7 danger sense allowed us to finish fast but unfortunately without scoring much due to taking out the sidekick villains.
First game was nuts.  We wrecked in under 20 minutes.  Second game made for a more challenging but, exciting match.
Quite an enjoyable melking. Fun setup and fun to use the villains stuff.

"3.5 to 4. I enjoyed it but I feel like there was nothing punishing about the game. I enjoy the games that are punishing but there still a chance to win."

column36: Now I'm glad I did not pick Invisible Woman last week. She seemed a perfect fit: KO against bindings, ability to get extra bystanders, sister to Human Torch and fellow translucent to Drax the Invisible. At the meantime Doc Ock have invited Venom for a feast of bystanders (with green and yellow synergy with Drax). Was also considering Thing for extra recruit, but got affraid of his high cost cards. Even this way HQ got clogged up for first third of the game with 6 and 8 cost cards, while always pulling max 5 recruit. Meantime the Avengers started the party in the city, with Thor, Cap and Hulk appearing in first 5 turns. They stayed for quite a while. Mid game was constant stress, just keeping the city safe (and being lucky with bystander and scheme twist draw). In the end with Venom's cards the avengers and later on Wolverine cards collected like 4-5 bystanders each. Combined that with being greedy and having the extra recruit I have ended up kidnapping all 53 bystanders by turn 32 or 33. And Venom did not eat any of them. (I did not get that card. Neither the InvWoman bystander one.) Maybe for the first time ever I got all 5 Drax artifacts, so that helped a lot in actually finishing the game. Sort of just in time. With 36 turns I am guessing I will be the slowest, but with all the bystanders and almost all villains in the victory pile, I expect that in VP/turn I may be in better half, so in the end I did not play a second round. (Despite this being a fun setup.)

Extra cheese got invisible woman’s focus 2 ko and vomitted his deck into the ko pile. Pizza tried to go hard black but extra cheese kept grabbing the draw cards.  But it worked out in the end. Die oden.
We didn't get hammered by bindings right at the beginning like the first game, so this was much more fun.
The luck of the shuffles was not in our favour. But we still had fun. Bindings were so hard to get rid of though and having wounds as well made it extra challenging.

Phasing and What If! Used Cloak & Dagger and Uatu to minimize the hero classes (reduce Raganarok power), and maximize Phasing and What If. Didn't get to live the dream with Cloak & Dagger and all the double cards, but I did have SO much fun manipulating the top of my deck for Phasing and What If playoffs. X-Men Noir were a great villain group choice for the Investigate it added, allowing additional top-deck manipulation. Felt like I got lucky shuffles in both games. In game 1 I hit tons of Scheme Twists and Master Strikes early when they were not hurtful. Game 2, they stayed in the deck. So much synergy was enjoyed (even with the limited Sidekicks), and it's a really fun setup to replay.

column36: It was fun to double down on phasing this game. Had an escape early on which I'm sure hurt my score. Was pretty lucky on one of the last scheme twists I drew...was really close to getting a "game over" moment but just squeaked by. lol.

This one was kind of hard. Attempt 1 managed to prevent escapes until the last few turns, when 1 escape happened just before victory. Attempt 2 just ended in a loss, with victory far from in sight. My plandidn't work out great either, although I think it sort of did, I just didn't anticipate how hard the Scheme was in advance. I chose Thor and Uatu, the Watcher for my Hero Choices as they are mostly ranged and/or covert, the two classes mostly present in the given Heroes. Speedball, Wiccan and Vision were not that strong, but just OK.

Early Twists didn't add a Villain to the CIty, but while playing it dawned on me that only Master Strikes and Bystanders gave some breathing room. Attempt 1 I focused on managing HQ to get Ragnarok to 8-10 Attack value and hit him whenever I could, from as early as turn 12 that started working. His tactics help to disrupt that plan, but with as much ranged in the Hero Deck as I had, this eventually worked out again. It felt restrictive to not be able to buy new cards anymore, but my decks were already strong enough to handle Ragnarok and buying cards would only potentially make him harder to hit again.

Giants of Jotunheim, while thematic, are not the most reliable KO Henchmen, as you sometimes don't have a discard when you fight them and they occasionally discard your strong cards as well. The Registration Enforcers and this Scheme were a hard combo. My strategy in attempt 1 to go for the Mastermind ASAP worked out better than trying to control the city firtst in Attempt 2.

I'm curious if someone else had stronger Hero Choices and a totally different experience of this game. I came up with the idea that Soulbind would have been very nice in this setup, but even with Doctor Strange Supreme and Gamora or Uatu, chances are likely you don't even get to buy the cards that let you Soulbind (9-12 cards in 70). The risk of adding Instinct to HQ would have to balance that Soulbinding prevents Villains from returning to the city.

All in all I found this a pretty hard setup, with City management being a continually increasing problem. I didn't end up with an empty city either, so left some points on the table. If I didn't end as soon as I did though, I think it would turn out to become a loss, so 1 escape will have to do. Turn 24 felt like a fast win with my Hero pool, although with stronger Heroes victory could probably come even faster.

Speedball and Wiccan are not my favourite heroes, although Wiccan and Vision at least work together pretty well with their Phasing and top deck guesses. I feel like they were chosen to partially increase difficulty with the Mastermind, with their Doubelsided cards providing 2 colours for the Mastermind, but it was left up to the player choice to add Strength and Instinct or not. I'm interested in what will happen in week 5. Many thanks to Kylah and Bageltop for making a great engaging tournament again. I'm starting to notice an increasing difficulty in the setups so far, so I'm not sure if I'm ready for next week.

I was terrified knowing I was facing off against Galactus. And I was kind of right to be. He chewed up two city spaces and his heralds are tough early game so 2 Stardust Villains escaped by turn 10 or so. 
But golly Warpath was a good pick. Player 1 recruited 2 endless endurances and always had more than 6 cards per turn. That combined with the 2s and 3s of the cuckoos AND the 2s of Warpath meant lots of attack and the final turn I had 25 attack, good for the final two hits on Galactus. 
Right hand wanted to go with a Shard deck, Combining Quasar and Adam Warlock, with Nova for an avenger boost, but the problem was I had several cards that affected Villains, and too often mid-late game I had no villains, just warlocks turning, which I can't assign Cosmic Threat too, or shatter, or burn shards to defeat. So it was less effective.
First time playing Adam Warlock, Quasar, and Stepford Cuckoos (Heroes) and Turn the Soul of Adam Warlock (Scheme).

tough game.  I'm lucky I pulled off a win in my first game, because I failed my second.  I barely stopped Adam Warlock from becoming corrupted. between bridgebuilders, subterranea and Galactus' strikes, the villains were always on the brink of escape. The Deathlok/Nova tech draw saved me and killed Galactus.  Pretty good setup.
I really enjoyed this game.  Having the Mastermind and the Scheme each doing different things with the Bystanders was fun.

Really enjoyed this. Had to play solo, but think my son would have loved it! Got some lucky Star Lord Artifacts that really helped build up shards. Thought all the bystanders coming out, each worth 3 VP made it fun. Will try to play this with my son after this week.
Both attempts had an escape (grrr) but piercing energy was fun on this one.  Was really fun, I'm looking forward to the insane scores other people will get on this one!

Mojo is always an interesting fight because you can't get around the Human shields. This generally slows the game down a bit where you have to manage the city and those human shields in the first few turns. 

We were able to get lucky with a pretty easy X-23 and Longshot deck early to eliminate the waves of threats early and berserk their way through some early deck limitations. This allowed the deck to get up to 10-12 attack a turn pretty effectively and mow down the city and then Mojo.

The other deck tried to take advantage of the ritual artifacts with Voodoo and Doctor Strange. It was a little slower to get going, but managed to effectively help enough in the mid-late game to really provide a balanced victory overall. Fantastic Four provided some unique help, especially with the Doctor Voodoo deck as it gave an additional use of the resources.

column36: This setup was a lot of fun. Thematically it worked well. I could picture Carnage sending Iron Man into space while he and the other heroes try to stop it. There was a lot of Feast which is scary, but really helped thin the decks fast. The the heroes were very focused around Strength cards so it was fun to lean into that. P1 went for an Instinct deck with a bit of Tech sprinkled in, but didn't get too developed. P2 leaned hard into the Strength and it really paid off with a huge penultimate turn that helped finish the game in under 20 turns. This is an interesting scheme. Its a timer scheme, but early twists can help more than they hurt. It probably would have been a lot harder without as many recruit focused heroes that can still deliver a punch.
The heroes i chose didn't really matter. Having access to the Prison Ship made it really easy to build a synergistic deck.

column36: i really Like the Plot of Betrayal Killmonger 6 cost card. I was able to rescue all the bystanders!!! that is a first for me! I was able to just keep villains dragging bystanders through the city! really enjoyed this run!!

Really fun setup. There was always stuff happening each turn. The puzzle factor with managing scheme twists, symbiote bonds, liberate, etc. was very engaging particularly having to decide where things go on a given turn gave us the feeling of control that you don't typically have. Daisy chaining attack off of liberate was also pretty cool. The required heroes worked really well with the setup and everything fit nicely. Not sure how great we'll do, but highly enjoyable.

I'm guessing you are trying to rotate through all masterminds and schemes or something but I kinda wish you didn't. Obviously, I still had fun. I like playing Legendary. But a game that's about optimization and not about winning just isn't fun. It's a very easy mastermind and scheme. Just a guaranteed win. Which, personally, isn't what I like. I know I'm bad at the game but being given this scheme really feels like it's just meant to remind me how bad I am. But I get it. Others prob enjoyed this more and it was still fun. Will prob get last cause I was slow though, idk.

column36: Fun playing against a rather weak MM in Red Skull.  I liked this because we never got super frustrated and mad at the game like we usually do, lol.
